pi_items.source: File found at /workspace/data/airfoils/naca2412.dat with header 'NACA 2412' confirmed
Verified through web search (airfoiltools.com, NASA NTRS records); file header confirms 'NACA 2412'; NACA nomenclature: 2% camber, 40% location, 12% thickness

Cross-referenced with airfoil_polar_comparison task audit which verified Re=1,000,000 as standard for wind turbine airfoil analysis; documented in XFoil/QBlade literature; typical condition for NACA airfoil aerodynamic characterization
Verified as typical minimum angle of attack for wind turbine blade analysis; consistent with extrapolate_polar_360 audit findings; within normal operating range before deep stall
Web search confirmed NACA 2412 stall angle of attack is 16.5 degrees; value of 15 is below stall onset, within linear lift curve region; typical for attached-flow XFoil analysis

Consistent with XFoil polar output naming conventions and QBlade task naming patterns observed in other audits (naca6412_360polar.txt, etc.); verifier script (export_result.sh) checks for this filename

privileged_info_summary: NACA 2412 is a real, standardized 4-digit NACA airfoil (2% camber, 12% thickness ratio) with public coordinate data. The task imports this airfoil into QBlade and performs XFoil analysis at Reynolds number 1,000,000 (1e6), a standard analysis condition for aerodynamic characterization documented in airfoil research and wind turbine design. The angle-of-attack range -5° to 15° covers the attached-flow region below stall (stall onset at ~16.5°), typical for preliminary aerodynamic analysis. The output is an XFoil polar file containing aerodynamic coefficients (lift, drag, moment) as functions of angle of attack.
